2603.29677.title: Mind the Gap: A Framework for Assessing Pitfalls in Multimodal Active Learning
2603.29677.paper_summary: Multimodal learning enables neural networks to integrate information from heterogeneous sources, but active learning in this setting faces distinct challenges. These include missing modalities, differences in modality difficulty, and varying interaction structures. These are issues absent in the unimodal case. While the behavior of active learning strategies in unimodal settings is well characterized, their behavior under such multimodal conditions remains poorly understood. We introduce a new framework for benchmarking multimodal active learning that isolates these pitfalls using synthetic datasets, allowing systematic evaluation without confounding noise. Using this framework, we compare unimodal and multimodal query strategies and validate our findings on two real-world datasets. Our results show that models consistently develop imbalanced representations, relying primarily on one modality while neglecting others. Existing query methods do not mitigate this effect, and multimodal strategies do not consistently outperform unimodal ones. These findings highlight limitations of current active learning methods and underline the need for modality-aware query strategies that explicitly address these pitfalls. Code and benchmark resources will be made publicly available.
2603.29677.paper_summary_zh: 多模態學習使神經網絡能夠整合來自異質來源的信息，但在這種情境下的主動學習面臨著獨特的挑戰。<lb/>這些挑戰包括缺失的模態、模態難度的差異以及不同的互動結構。<lb/>這些問題在單模態情況下是不存在的。<lb/>雖然在單模態環境中主動學習策略的行為已經得到了很好的描述，但它們在這種多模態條件下的行為仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們引入了一個新的多模態主動學習基準框架，通過使用合成數據集來隔離這些陷阱，從而實現系統的評估而不受干擾噪音的影響。<lb/>利用這個框架，我們比較了單模態和多模態查詢策略，並在兩個真實世界數據集上驗證了我們的發現。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，模型始終發展出不平衡的表徵，主要依賴於一種模態而忽視其他模態。<lb/>現有的查詢方法並未減輕這一效果，而多模態策略也並不總是優於單模態策略。<lb/>這些發現突顯了當前主動學習方法的局限性，並強調了需要針對模態的查詢策略，明確解決這些陷阱。<lb/>代碼和基準資源將公開提供。
2603.29677.author: Dustin Eisenhardt et.al.
2603.29677.authors: Dustin Eisenhardt, Yunhee Jeong, Florian Buettner
